mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving corporate earnings expectations higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, which compresses profit margins and generates negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate earnings and force central banks to tighten policy, generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals weakening domestic demand momentum, triggering rapid short-term equity sell-offs.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0 and RSAFS monthly growth < 0.5
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of observations when both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fall below specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing below its 12-month average is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.
